pattern: The best empty seat is either the middle of the longest interior gap between two people or the very end of a leading or trailing run of empty seats, so one scan of gap lengths suffices.
hints: Sitting in the middle of a long empty stretch maximises the distance, but a stretch touching either end behaves differently from one bounded by people on both sides.
For a gap of empty seats bounded by people on both sides, the best you can do is half its length; for a run of empties at the start or end you can reach its full length.

Track the index of the previous occupied seat: between two occupied seats the candidate is (gap)/2, while leading empties give the first occupied index and trailing empties give length-1-minus-last-occupied; take the maximum.

edge_inputs.description: interior gap whose midpoint beats the unit-distance options
trailing empties give the full run length
leading empties measured to the first person, not halved
people at both ends so only interior midpoints count
adjacent people leave only unit gaps
longer leading run versus an interior gap

stress.description: long random seat row guaranteed to contain at least one person and one empty seat
stress.note: forces both a person and an empty seat to exist
